source_unit.source_text:
\begin{corollary}
\label{cor.lgv.catalan-hankel-det-0}Let $k\in\mathbb{N}$. Recall the Catalan
numbers $c_{n}=\dfrac{1}{n+1}\dbinom{2n}{n}$ for all $n\in\mathbb{N}$. Then,%
\[
\det\left(  \left(  c_{i+j-2}\right)  _{1\leq i\leq k,\ 1\leq j\leq k}\right)
=\det\left(
\begin{array}
[c]{cccc}%
c_{0} & c_{1} & \cdots & c_{k-1}\\
c_{1} & c_{2} & \cdots & c_{k}\\
\vdots & \vdots & \ddots & \vdots\\
c_{k-1} & c_{k} & \cdots & c_{2k-2}%
\end{array}
\right)  =1.
\]

\end{corollary}

project_declarations.lean_snippet:
theorem lgv_nonpermutable {k : ℕ} (w : ArcWeight integerLattice K)
    (A B : kVertex (ℤ × ℤ) k)
    (hxA : xDecreasing A) (hyA : yIncreasing A)
    (hxB : xDecreasing B) (hyB : yIncreasing B) :
    (pathWeightMatrix integerLattice_pathFinite w A B).det =
      nipatWeightSum integerLattice_pathFinite w A B (Equiv.refl (Fin k))
project_declarations.name: LGV.lgv_nonpermutable

project_declarations.lean_snippet:
/-- First Jacobi-Trudi formula (Theorem thm.sf.jt-h)
    Label: thm.sf.jt-h

    s_{λ/μ} = det((h_{λᵢ - μⱼ - i + j})_{1 ≤ i,j ≤ M})

    The proof uses the Lindström-Gessel-Viennot lemma with appropriate
    source and target vertices in the integer lattice ℤ².

    ## Proof Strategy

    **Step 1: Set up the LGV framework**
    Define source vertices Aᵢ = (μᵢ - i, 1) and target vertices Bⱼ = (λⱼ - j, N) in ℤ².
    The path weight matrix M_{i,j} = ∑_{p : Aᵢ → Bⱼ} w(p) where w(p) is the product
    of x_h for each east-step at height h.

    **Step 2: Identify M with the Jacobi-Trudi matrix**
    By `pathWeightSum_eq_hsymm`, M_{i,j} = h_{λⱼ - μᵢ - j + i} = h_{λⱼ - μᵢ - (j - i)}.
    Note: The textbook uses 1-indexed notation; our Lean formalization uses 0-indexed.
    The matrix entry (i,j) is h_{λᵢ - μⱼ - i + j} in the textbook convention.

    **Step 3: Apply the LGV lemma**
    By `lgv_nonpermutable` (Corollary cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np), since the source and
    target vertices satisfy the sorting conditions (x-decreasing, y-increasing),
    we have:
      det(M) = ∑_{nipats from 𝐀 to 𝐁} w(nipat)
    where the sum is only over non-intersecting path tuples (nipats).

    **Step 4: Establish the nipat-SSYT bijection**
    By `nipat_ssyt_bijection`, there is a weight-preserving bijection between
    nipats from 𝐀 to 𝐁 and SSYT of skew shape λ/μ:
    - The nipat (p₁, ..., pₖ) maps to the tableau T where row i contains
      the heights of east-steps in path pᵢ (in weakly increasing order).
    - Non-intersection of paths ensures column-strictness of the tableau.
    - The weight w(nipat) = ∏ᵢ w(pᵢ) equals the monomial x^T.

    **Step 5: Conclude**
    det(M) = ∑_{nipats} w(nipat) = ∑_{SSYT T of shape λ/μ} x^T = s_{λ/μ}

    ## Key Lemmas Required

@[ext]
lemma PathTuple.ext {V : Type*} [DecidableEq V] {D : SimpleDigraph V} {k : ℕ}
    {A B : kVertex V k} (pt1 pt2 : PathTuple D k A B)
    (h : ∀ i, pt1.paths i = pt2.paths i) : pt1 = pt2 := by
  cases pt1; cases pt2
  simp only [PathTuple.mk.injEq]
  ext i
  exact h i
local_file_predecessor_declarations.name: PathTuple.ext

def dyckDigraph : SimpleDigraph (ℤ × ℕ) where
  arc u v := (v.1 = u.1 + 1 ∧ v.2 = u.2 + 1) ∨
             (v.1 = u.1 + 1 ∧ v.2 + 1 = u.2 ∧ 0 < u.2)
  arc_irrefl := by
    intro ⟨x, y⟩
    simp only [not_or, not_and]
    constructor <;> omega
local_file_predecessor_declarations.name: dyckDigraph

local_file_predecessor_declarations.lean_snippet:
private lemma dyckDigraph_arc_translate (d : ℤ) (u v : ℤ × ℕ) :
    dyckDigraph.arc u v ↔ dyckDigraph.arc (translateVertex d u) (translateVertex d v) := by
  unfold dyckDigraph translateVertex
  simp only
  constructor
  · intro h
    rcases h with ⟨hx, hy⟩ | ⟨hx, hy, hpos⟩
    · left; constructor <;> omega
    · right; constructor; omega; constructor; omega; exact hpos
  · intro h
    rcases h with ⟨hx, hy⟩ | ⟨hx, hy, hpos⟩
    · left; constructor <;> omega
    · right; refine ⟨?_, ?_, hpos⟩ <;> omega
local_file_predecessor_declarations.name: dyckDigraph_arc_translate

local_file_predecessor_declarations.benchmark_honesty: Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.
local_file_predecessor_declarations.lean_snippet:
def translatePath (d : ℤ) (p : SimpleDigraph.Path dyckDigraph) :
    SimpleDigraph.Path dyckDigraph where
  vertices := p.vertices.map (translateVertex d)
  nonempty := by simp [p.nonempty]
  arcs_valid := by
    intro k hk
    simp only [List.length_map] at hk
    have hk' : k < p.vertices.length := Nat.lt_of_succ_lt hk
    have hk'' : k + 1 < p.vertices.length := hk

    have h1 : (p.vertices.map (translateVertex d)).get ⟨k, by simp; exact hk'⟩ =
              translateVertex d (p.vertices.get ⟨k, hk'⟩) := by
      simp [List.get_eq_getElem]
    have h2 : (p.vertices.map (translateVertex d)).get ⟨k + 1, by simp; exact hk''⟩ =
              translateVertex d (p.vertices.get ⟨k + 1, hk''⟩) := by
      simp [List.get_eq_getElem]
    rw [h1, h2]
    rw [← dyckDigraph_arc_translate d]
    exact p.arcs_valid k hk
local_file_predecessor_declarations.name: translatePath

local_file_predecessor_declarations.benchmark_honesty: Uses post-hoc target file/line placement only to select earlier declarations; current-unit aligned/referencing declarations are omitted.

private lemma translatePath_start (d : ℤ) (p : SimpleDigraph.Path dyckDigraph) :
    (translatePath d p).start = translateVertex d p.start := by
  unfold translatePath SimpleDigraph.Path.start translateVertex
  simp only [List.head_map]
local_file_predecessor_declarations.name: translatePath_start

private lemma translatePath_finish (d : ℤ) (p : SimpleDigraph.Path dyckDigraph) :
    (translatePath d p).finish = translateVertex d p.finish := by
  unfold translatePath SimpleDigraph.Path.finish translateVertex
  simp only [List.getLast_map]
local_file_predecessor_declarations.name: translatePath_finish

local_file_predecessor_declarations.lean_snippet:
private lemma translatePath_neg (d : ℤ) (p : SimpleDigraph.Path dyckDigraph) :
    translatePath (-d) (translatePath d p) = p := by
  simp only [translatePath, List.map_map]
  congr 1
  conv_rhs => rw [← List.map_id p.vertices]
  congr 1
  funext v
  simp only [Function.comp_apply, translateVertex]
  ext
  · simp only [id_eq]; ring
  · rfl
local_file_predecessor_declarations.name: translatePath_neg

local_file_predecessor_declarations.lean_snippet:
lemma translatePath_injective (d : ℤ) : Function.Injective (translatePath d) := by
  intro p q hpq
  have h := congrArg (translatePath (-d)) hpq
  simp only [translatePath_neg] at h
  exact h
local_file_predecessor_declarations.name: translatePath_injective

local_file_predecessor_declarations.lean_snippet:
theorem dyck_paths_shifted_eq_catalan (i j : ℕ) :
    (pathsFromTo dyckDigraph dyckDigraph_pathFinite (-(2 * i : ℤ), 0) (2 * j, 0)).card =
      catalan (i + j) := by

  have equiv : (pathsFromTo dyckDigraph dyckDigraph_pathFinite (-(2 * i : ℤ), 0) (2 * j, 0)) ≃
               (pathsFromTo dyckDigraph dyckDigraph_pathFinite (0, 0) (2 * (i + j), 0)) := by
    refine ⟨?toFun, ?invFun, ?left_inv, ?right_inv⟩
    case toFun =>
      intro ⟨p, hp⟩
      refine ⟨translatePath (2 * i) p, ?_⟩
      rw [mem_pathsFromTo_dyck] at hp ⊢
      constructor
      · rw [translatePath_start, hp.1]
        simp [translateVertex]
      · rw [translatePath_finish, hp.2]
        simp [translateVertex]
        ring
    case invFun =>
      intro ⟨p, hp⟩
      refine ⟨translatePath (-(2 * i : ℤ)) p, ?_⟩
      rw [mem_pathsFromTo_dyck] at hp ⊢
      constructor
      · rw [translatePath_start, hp.1]
        simp [translateVertex]
      · rw [translatePath_finish, hp.2]
        simp [translateVertex]
        ring
    case left_inv =>
      intro ⟨p, hp⟩
      simp only [Subtype.mk.injEq]
      exact translatePath_neg (2 * i) p
    case right_inv =>
      intro ⟨p, hp⟩
      simp only [Subtype.mk.injEq]
      have h : translatePath (2 * ↑i) (translatePath (-(2 * ↑i)) p) = p := by
        have := translatePath_neg (-(2 * i : ℤ)) p
        simp only [neg_neg] at this
        exact this
      exact h
  calc (pathsFromTo dyckDigraph dyckDigraph_pathFinite (-(2 * i : ℤ), 0) (2 * j, 0)).card
local_file_predecessor_declarations.name: dyck_paths_shifted_eq_catalan

local_file_predecessor_declarations.lean_snippet:
theorem catalanHankelMatrix_eq_pathWeightMatrix (k : ℕ) :
    catalanHankelMatrix k =
    pathWeightMatrix dyckDigraph_pathFinite dyckUnitArcWeight
      (fun i : Fin k => (-(2 * (i : ℤ)), 0))
      (fun j : Fin k => (2 * (j : ℤ), 0)) := by
  ext i j
  simp only [catalanHankelMatrix, pathWeightMatrix, Matrix.of_apply]
  rw [pathWeightSum_dyckUnitArcWeight]

  rw [← dyck_paths_shifted_eq_catalan]
local_file_predecessor_declarations.name: catalanHankelMatrix_eq_pathWeightMatrix

source_unit.source_text:
\begin{corollary}
[LGV lemma, nonpermutable lattice weight version]\label{cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np}%
Consider the setting of Theorem \ref{thm.lgv.kpaths.wt}, but additionally
assume that%
\begin{align}
\operatorname*{x}\left(  A_{1}\right)   &  \geq\operatorname*{x}\left(
A_{2}\right)  \geq\cdots\geq\operatorname*{x}\left(  A_{k}\right)
;\label{eq.cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np.xA}\\
\operatorname*{y}\left(  A_{1}\right)   &  \leq\operatorname*{y}\left(
A_{2}\right)  \leq\cdots\leq\operatorname*{y}\left(  A_{k}\right)
;\label{eq.cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np.yA}\\
\operatorname*{x}\left(  B_{1}\right)   &  \geq\operatorname*{x}\left(
B_{2}\right)  \geq\cdots\geq\operatorname*{x}\left(  B_{k}\right)
;\label{eq.cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np.xB}\\
\operatorname*{y}\left(  B_{1}\right)   &  \leq\operatorname*{y}\left(
B_{2}\right)  \leq\cdots\leq\operatorname*{y}\left(  B_{k}\right)  .
\label{eq.cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np.yB}%
\end{align}
Here, $\operatorname*{x}\left(  P\right)  $ and $\operatorname*{y}\left(
P\right)  $ denote the two coordinates of any point $P\in\mathbb{Z}^{2}$.

Then, there are no nipats from $\mathbf{A}$ to $\sigma\left(  \mathbf{B}%
\right)  $ when $\sigma\in S_{k}$ is not the identity permutation
$\operatorname*{id}\in S_{k}$. Therefore, the claim of Theorem
\ref{thm.lgv.kpaths.wt} simplifies to
\begin{align}
&  \det\left(  \left(  \sum_{p:A_{i}\rightarrow B_{j}}w\left(  p\right)
\right)  _{1\leq i\leq k,\ 1\leq j\leq k}\right) \nonumber\\
&  =\sum_{\substack{\mathbf{p}\text{ is a nipat}\\\text{from }\mathbf{A}\text{
to }\mathbf{B}}}w\left(  \mathbf{p}\right)  .
\label{eq.cor.lgv.kpaths.wt-np.claim}%
\end{align}

\end{corollary}

source_unit.source_text:
\begin{corollary}
\label{cor.lgv.binom-det-nonneg}Let $k\in\mathbb{N}$. Let $a_{1},a_{2}%
,\ldots,a_{k}$ and $b_{1},b_{2},\ldots,b_{k}$ be nonnegative integers such
that
\[
a_{1}\geq a_{2}\geq\cdots\geq a_{k}\ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \text{and}%
\ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ \ b_{1}\geq b_{2}\geq\cdots\geq b_{k}.
\]
Then,%
\[
\det\left(  \left(  \dbinom{a_{i}}{b_{j}}\right)  _{1\leq i\leq k,\ 1\leq
j\leq k}\right)  \geq0.
\]

\end{corollary}
